## Bankruptcy in Kearney, MO - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/kearney.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Kearney, MO

Bankruptcy in Kearney, MO

Kearney is a northern Clay County community known as the birthplace of outlaw Jesse James. The city has experienced rapid suburban growth and is a popular choice for families seeking newer housing developments north of Kansas City, with a population of approximately 10,700.

Key Filing Information

County

Clay County

Population: 10,700 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

64060

Files in W.D. Mo.

Missouri Exemptions in Kearney

PropertyMissouri Exemption

Homestead$15,000

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Kearney, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity. Same metro area. Vastly different outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Kearney

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313

Attorney fees: $1,000-$2,000 (Ch. 7); $2,500-$4,000 (Ch. 13) in KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024)

Compare: Kansas Side

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024)

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Clay County Administration Building - 1 Courthouse Square, Liberty, MO 64068

Legal Aid of Western Missouri - 1125 Grand Blvd #1900, Kansas City, MO 64106

Missouri 211 (dial 2-1-1) - Free referrals to local services

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Kearney, MO cases?

Kearney is in Clay County, filing in the Western District of Missouri at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106.

What exemptions apply in Kearney, MO?

Missouri exemptions: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Full guide.

What about Kearney specifically?

Kearney's newer housing developments mean many homeowners have mortgages close to or exceeding home values, which can actually make Chapter 7 more feasible since there is less equity to protect.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Missouri for your specific circumstances.

## Bankruptcy in Lansing, KS - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/lansing.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Lansing, KS

Bankruptcy in Lansing, KS

Lansing is a city in Leavenworth County, Kansas, along the Missouri River north of Kansas City. It is home to the Lansing Correctional Facility and the Kansas Soldiers' Home, with a significant military-connected population.

Key Filing Information

County

Leavenworth County

Population: 12,300 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66043

All file in D. Kan.

Kansas Exemptions in Lansing

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUnlimited (1 acre urban, 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit (reasonable necessity)

WildcardNone (not needed with unlimited homestead)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions. Kansas exemptions are among the most generous in the nation.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Lansing, KS can protect unlimited home equity and up to $20,000-$40,000 in vehicle equity. Someone across the state line in Missouri can protect only $15,000 in home equity and $3,000 in vehicle equity. Same metro area, dramatically different bankruptcy outcomes.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Lansing

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - lower than the Missouri side

Compare: Missouri Side

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - significantly higher dismissal rate

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Leavenworth County Courthouse - 601 S 3rd St, Leavenworth, KS 66048

Kansas Legal Services - 712 S Kansas Ave, Topeka, KS 66603 (serves statewide)

Kansas 211 (dial 2-1-1) - Free referrals to local services including financial counseling

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Lansing, KS cases?

Lansing is in Leavenworth County, which files in the District of Kansas, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101.

What exemptions apply in Lansing, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply. Unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban), $20,000 vehicle ($40,000 married), fully exempt retirement. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

How does Lansing compare to Missouri suburbs?

Kansas residents benefit from an unlimited homestead exemption, while Missouri suburbs are limited to $15,000. Kansas vehicle exemption is $20,000/$40,000 versus Missouri s $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Missouri instead of Kansas?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Missouri, you must reside there at least 730 days before Missouri exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Lansing specifically?

Lansing has a significant military-connected population. Active service members have extra bankruptcy protections under the SCRA. All Lansing residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead and $20,000 vehicle exemption.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Kansas for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

## Bankruptcy in Leawood, KS - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/leawood.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Leawood, KS

Bankruptcy in Leawood, KS

Leawood is an affluent suburb in southeastern Johnson County with a population of about 34,700. It features upscale shopping, dining, and some of the highest property values in the KC metro.

Key Filing Information

County

Johnson County

Population: 34,659 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City, KS Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66206, 66209, 66211, 66224

All file in D. Kan.

Kansas Exemptions in Leawood

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUNLIMITED (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit on necessary items

Personal Property$20,000 total (single) / $40,000 (married)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions.

The Kansas Advantage

Leawood residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption. Across the state line in Missouri, homeowners can protect only $15,000 in home equity. This is one of the most dramatic exemption differences in the country.

Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married). Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Leawood

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower than Missouri side

Compare: Missouri Side

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - higher dismissal rate across the state line

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Leawood City Hall - 4800 Town Center Dr, Leawood, KS 66211

Kansas Legal Services - 712 S Kansas Ave #200, Topeka, KS 66603 (statewide intake)

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Leawood, KS cases?

Leawood is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Leawood, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Leawood residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Leawood specifically?

Leawood has some of the most expensive homes in the Kansas City area. Thanks to Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption, even Leawood homeowners with hundreds of thousands in equity can protect their primary residence. This is perhaps the starkest contrast with Missouri's $15,000 cap.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Kansas for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

## Bankruptcy in Lee's Summit, MO - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/lees-summit.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Lee's Summit, MO

Bankruptcy in Lee's Summit, MO

Lee's Summit is one of the fastest-growing cities in Missouri, located southeast of downtown Kansas City. With a population exceeding 101,000, it is a major suburban center with above-average home values.

Key Filing Information

County

Jackson County

Population: 101,168 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

64063, 64064, 64081, 64082, 64086

All file in W.D. Mo.

Missouri Exemptions in Lee's Summit

PropertyMissouri Exemption

Homestead$15,000

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Lee's Summit, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity (up to 1 acre urban). Same metro area. Same housing market. Vastly different bankruptcy outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Lee's Summit

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - roughly 4 in 10 Ch. 13 cases dismissed

Compare: Kansas Side

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower dismissal rate

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Lee's Summit Municipal Court - 220 SE Green St, Lee's Summit, MO 64063

Legal Aid of Western Missouri - 1125 Grand Blvd #1900, Kansas City, MO 64106

Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Greater KC - 4049 Pennsylvania Ave #200, Kansas City, MO 64111

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Lee's Summit, MO cases?

Lee's Summit is in Jackson County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. The W.D. Mo. has a 40.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Lee's Summit, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does Lee's Summit compare to Kansas suburbs for bankruptcy?

Lee's Summit residents are subject to Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs across the state line have an unlimited homestead exemption and a $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file in the district where you live. Lee's Summit residents must file in the Western District of Missouri. You cannot choose Kansas to get better exemptions. If you move to Kansas, you must live there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Lee's Summit specifically?

Lee's Summit has higher median home values than the KC metro average. This means many homeowners have equity well above Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption, making Chapter 13 particularly important for homeowners here.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Missouri for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Bankruptcy in Lenexa, KS

Lenexa is a suburb in central Johnson County with a population of about 57,400. It is home to the City Center development and a growing tech and business corridor along I-435.

Key Filing Information

County

Johnson County

Population: 57,434 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City, KS Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

All file in D. Kan.

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUNLIMITED (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit on necessary items

Personal Property$20,000 total (single) / $40,000 (married)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions.

The Kansas Advantage

Lenexa residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption. Across the state line in Missouri, homeowners can protect only $15,000 in home equity. This is one of the most dramatic exemption differences in the country.

Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married). Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Lenexa

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Lenexa, KS cases?

Lenexa is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Lenexa, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Lenexa residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Lenexa specifically?

Lenexa residents benefit from Kansas's generous exemptions. A single filer can protect $20,000 in vehicle equity - compared to just $3,000 across the state line in Missouri.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Kansas for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Check Your Eligibility

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

## Bankruptcy in Liberty, MO - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/liberty.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Liberty, MO

Bankruptcy in Liberty, MO

Liberty is the county seat of Clay County, located north of Kansas City. Home to William Jewell College, it has a population of about 31,500 and serves as a suburban hub for the northern KC metro.

Key Filing Information

County

Clay County

Population: 31,507 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

64068, 64069

All file in W.D. Mo.

Missouri Exemptions in Liberty

PropertyMissouri Exemption

Homestead$15,000

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Liberty, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity (up to 1 acre urban). Same metro area. Same housing market. Vastly different bankruptcy outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Liberty

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - roughly 4 in 10 Ch. 13 cases dismissed

Compare: Kansas Side

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower dismissal rate

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Clay County Administration Building - 1 Courthouse Square, Liberty, MO 64068

Legal Aid of Western Missouri - 1125 Grand Blvd #1900, Kansas City, MO 64106

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Liberty, MO cases?

Liberty is in Clay County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. The W.D. Mo. has a 40.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Liberty, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does Liberty compare to Kansas suburbs for bankruptcy?

Liberty residents are subject to Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs across the state line have an unlimited homestead exemption and a $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file in the district where you live. Liberty residents must file in the Western District of Missouri. You cannot choose Kansas to get better exemptions. If you move to Kansas, you must live there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Liberty specifically?

Liberty is in Clay County, which also files through the Western District of Missouri. Clay County has experienced rapid growth, meaning many residents purchased homes at higher prices and may face different equity considerations.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Missouri for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

## Bankruptcy in Merriam, KS - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/merriam.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Merriam, KS

Bankruptcy in Merriam, KS

Merriam is a small suburb in northeastern Johnson County with a population of about 11,200. Located along I-35 and Shawnee Mission Parkway, it is a compact community with easy access to the broader KC metro.

Key Filing Information

County

Johnson County

Population: 11,210 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City, KS Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66202, 66203

All file in D. Kan.

Kansas Exemptions in Merriam

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUNLIMITED (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit on necessary items

Personal Property$20,000 total (single) / $40,000 (married)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions.

The Kansas Advantage

Merriam residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption. Across the state line in Missouri, homeowners can protect only $15,000 in home equity. This is one of the most dramatic exemption differences in the country.

Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married). Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Merriam

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower than Missouri side

Compare: Missouri Side

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - higher dismissal rate across the state line

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Merriam City Hall - 9001 W 62nd St, Merriam, KS 66202

Kansas Legal Services - 712 S Kansas Ave #200, Topeka, KS 66603 (statewide intake)

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Merriam, KS cases?

Merriam is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Merriam, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Merriam residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Merriam specifically?

Merriam's central location makes it easy to access the District of Kansas courthouse at 500 State Ave in Kansas City, KS. Filing in Kansas means access to the unlimited homestead exemption and $20,000 vehicle exemption.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Kansas for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Mission, KS cases?

Mission is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Mission, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Mission residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Mission specifically?

Mission sits near the Kansas-Missouri border. Despite being minutes from Missouri, Mission residents file under Kansas law with its far more generous exemptions - unlimited homestead protection versus Missouri's $15,000 cap.

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » North Kansas City, MO

Bankruptcy in North Kansas City, MO

North Kansas City is a small but economically significant city in Clay County, situated directly north of the Missouri River from downtown Kansas City. Despite its small population of roughly 4,800, it hosts a major commercial and industrial tax base and is home to the former Cerner (now Oracle Health) campus.

Key Filing Information

County

Clay County

Population: 4,800 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

64116, 64117

All file in W.D. Mo.

PropertyMissouri Exemption

Homestead$15,000

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

The State Line Difference

Someone in North Kansas City, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity (up to 1 acre urban). Same metro area. Same housing market. Vastly different bankruptcy outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in North Kansas City

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Missouri 211 (dial 2-1-1) - Free referrals to local services including financial counseling

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles North Kansas City, MO cases?

North Kansas City is in Clay County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. The W.D. Mo. has a 40.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in North Kansas City, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does North Kansas City compare to Kansas suburbs for bankruptcy?

North Kansas City residents are subject to Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs across the state line have an unlimited homestead exemption and a $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file in the district where you live. North Kansas City residents must file in the Western District of Missouri. You cannot choose Kansas to get better exemptions. If you move to Kansas, you must live there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about North Kansas City specifically?

North Kansas City residents often benefit from the city's low property tax rates, but Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption means even modest home equity may not be fully protected in a Chapter 7 case.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas: $20,000 / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Oak Grove

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Oak Grove, MO cases?

Oak Grove is in Jackson County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106.

What exemptions apply in Oak Grove, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply. $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, $600 wildcard plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

How does Oak Grove compare to Kansas suburbs?

Oak Grove residents face Missouri s $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs have unlimited homestead and $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Kansas, you must reside there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Oak Grove specifically?

Oak Grove has more affordable housing than many KC suburbs. This can be an advantage - more residents may have equity within Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption limit, making Chapter 7 a viable option.

## Bankruptcy in Odessa, MO - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/odessa.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Odessa, MO

Bankruptcy in Odessa, MO

Odessa is a small city in Lafayette County along I-70, roughly 40 miles east of Kansas City. It serves as a rural-suburban transition community with affordable housing and easy highway access to the KC metro.

Key Filing Information

County

Lafayette County

Population: 5,200 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

64076

All file in W.D. Mo.

Missouri Exemptions in Odessa

PropertyMissouri Exemption

Homestead$15,000

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Odessa, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity. Same metro area. Vastly different outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas: $20,000 / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Odessa

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - roughly 4 in 10 Ch. 13 cases dismissed

Compare: Kansas Side

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower dismissal rate

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Lafayette County Courthouse - 1001 Main St, Lexington, MO 64067

Legal Aid of Western Missouri - 1125 Grand Blvd #1900, Kansas City, MO 64106

Missouri 211 (dial 2-1-1) - Free referrals to local services including financial counseling

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Odessa, MO cases?

Odessa is in Lafayette County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106.

What exemptions apply in Odessa, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply. $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, $600 wildcard plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

How does Odessa compare to Kansas suburbs?

Odessa residents face Missouri s $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs have unlimited homestead and $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Kansas, you must reside there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Odessa specifically?

Odessa has some of the most affordable housing in the KC commuter area. Most residents will find their home equity falls within Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption, making Chapter 7 a straightforward option for many.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Missouri for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Check Your Eligibility

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

## Bankruptcy in Olathe, KS - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/olathe.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Olathe, KS

Bankruptcy in Olathe, KS

Olathe is the county seat of Johnson County and the fourth-largest city in Kansas, with a population exceeding 141,000. It is a fast-growing suburb with a strong economy and expanding residential areas.

Key Filing Information

County

Johnson County

Population: 141,290 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City, KS Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66051, 66061, 66062, 66063

All file in D. Kan.

Kansas Exemptions in Olathe

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUNLIMITED (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit on necessary items

Personal Property$20,000 total (single) / $40,000 (married)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions.

The Kansas Advantage

Olathe residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption. Across the state line in Missouri, homeowners can protect only $15,000 in home equity. This is one of the most dramatic exemption differences in the country.

Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married). Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Olathe

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower than Missouri side

Compare: Missouri Side

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - higher dismissal rate across the state line

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Johnson County Courthouse - 100 N Kansas Ave, Olathe, KS 66061

Kansas Legal Services - 712 S Kansas Ave #200, Topeka, KS 66603 (statewide intake)

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Olathe, KS cases?

Olathe is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Olathe, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Olathe residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Olathe specifically?

Olathe is the Johnson County seat and home to many county services. Kansas filers benefit from significantly higher exemptions than their Missouri neighbors - unlimited homestead and $20,000 vehicle protection versus Missouri's $15,000 and $3,000 respectively.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Kansas for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

## Bankruptcy in Ottawa, KS - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/ottawa.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Ottawa, KS

Bankruptcy in Ottawa, KS

Ottawa is the county seat of Franklin County, Kansas, along I-35 about 50 miles southwest of Kansas City. Home to Ottawa University, it is a regional center with a blend of college-town and agricultural-community character.

Key Filing Information

County

Franklin County

Population: 12,700 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66067

All file in D. Kan.

Kansas Exemptions in Ottawa

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUnlimited (1 acre urban, 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit (reasonable necessity)

WildcardNone (not needed with unlimited homestead)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions. Kansas exemptions are among the most generous in the nation.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Ottawa, KS can protect unlimited home equity and up to $20,000-$40,000 in vehicle equity. Someone across the state line in Missouri can protect only $15,000 in home equity and $3,000 in vehicle equity. Same metro area, dramatically different bankruptcy outcomes.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Ottawa

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - lower than the Missouri side

Compare: Missouri Side

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - significantly higher dismissal rate

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Franklin County Courthouse - 301 S Main St, Ottawa, KS 66067

Kansas Legal Services - 712 S Kansas Ave, Topeka, KS 66603 (serves statewide)

Kansas 211 (dial 2-1-1) - Free referrals to local services including financial counseling

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Ottawa, KS cases?

Ottawa is in Franklin County, which files in the District of Kansas, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101.

What exemptions apply in Ottawa, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply. Unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban), $20,000 vehicle ($40,000 married), fully exempt retirement. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

How does Ottawa compare to Missouri suburbs?

Kansas residents benefit from an unlimited homestead exemption, while Missouri suburbs are limited to $15,000. Kansas vehicle exemption is $20,000/$40,000 versus Missouri s $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Missouri instead of Kansas?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Missouri, you must reside there at least 730 days before Missouri exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Ottawa specifically?

Ottawa is the Franklin County seat, about 50 miles from KC. Kansas bankruptcy exemptions apply - unlimited homestead and $20,000 vehicle. These protections are the same whether you live in Overland Park or Ottawa.

Bankruptcy in Overland Park, KS

Overland Park is the second-largest city in Kansas and the largest suburb in the Kansas City metro area, with a population of nearly 200,000. It is a major business center with corporate headquarters and a highly educated workforce.

Key Filing Information

County

Johnson County

Population: 197,238 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City, KS Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66204, 66207, 66209, 66210, 66211, 66212, 66213, 66214, 66221, 66223, 66224, 66225, 66251

All file in D. Kan.

HomesteadUNLIMITED (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit on necessary items

Personal Property$20,000 total (single) / $40,000 (married)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions.

The Kansas Advantage

Overland Park residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption. Across the state line in Missouri, homeowners can protect only $15,000 in home equity. This is one of the most dramatic exemption differences in the country.

Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married). Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Overland Park

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Overland Park, KS cases?

Overland Park is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Overland Park, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Overland Park residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Overland Park specifically?

Overland Park has some of the highest home values in the KC metro. Because Kansas offers an unlimited homestead exemption (up to 1 acre urban), even Overland Park homeowners with significant equity can protect their home in bankruptcy. This is a dramatic advantage over the Missouri side.

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Paola, KS cases?

Paola is in Miami County, which files in the District of Kansas, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101.

What exemptions apply in Paola, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply. Unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban), $20,000 vehicle ($40,000 married), fully exempt retirement. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

How does Paola compare to Missouri suburbs?

Kansas residents benefit from an unlimited homestead exemption, while Missouri suburbs are limited to $15,000. Kansas vehicle exemption is $20,000/$40,000 versus Missouri s $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Missouri instead of Kansas?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Missouri, you must reside there at least 730 days before Missouri exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Paola specifically?

Paola is the Miami County seat with affordable housing. Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption means your entire home equity is protected. Combined with the $20,000 vehicle exemption, Kansas filers have significantly better protections than Missouri.

Bankruptcy in Parkville, MO

Parkville is a historic river town in Platte County along the Missouri River bluffs, home to Park University and known for its charming downtown arts district. It is consistently ranked among Missouri's best small cities for quality of life, with a population of about 7,200.

Key Filing Information

County

Platte County

Population: 7,200 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

Files in W.D. Mo.

Vehicle$3,000

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Parkville, MO cases?

Parkville is in Platte County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. The W.D. Mo. has a 40.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Parkville, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does Parkville compare to Kansas suburbs for bankruptcy?

Parkville residents are subject to Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs across the state line have an unlimited homestead exemption and a $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file in the district where you live. Parkville residents must file in the Western District of Missouri. You cannot choose Kansas to get better exemptions. If you move to Kansas, you must live there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Parkville specifically?

Parkville's real estate values tend to be above the KC metro average. With Missouri's low $15,000 homestead exemption, homeowners with significant equity may find Chapter 7 risky and should carefully evaluate Chapter 13 as an alternative.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Missouri for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Check Your Eligibility

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

## Bankruptcy in Platte City, MO - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/platte-city.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Platte City, MO

Bankruptcy in Platte City, MO

Platte City is the county seat of Platte County, located northwest of Kansas City near KCI Airport. It has experienced rapid growth driven by airport-area development and new residential subdivisions.

Key Filing Information

County

Platte County

Population: 5,100 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

64079

All file in W.D. Mo.

Missouri Exemptions in Platte City

PropertyMissouri Exemption

Homestead$15,000

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

The State Line Difference

Someone in Platte City, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity. Same metro area. Vastly different outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas: $20,000 / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Platte City

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - roughly 4 in 10 Ch. 13 cases dismissed

Compare: Kansas Side

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower dismissal rate

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Platte County Courthouse - 415 Third St, Platte City, MO 64079

Legal Aid of Western Missouri - 1125 Grand Blvd #1900, Kansas City, MO 64106

Missouri 211 (dial 2-1-1) - Free referrals to local services including financial counseling

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Platte City, MO cases?

Platte City is in Platte County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106.

What exemptions apply in Platte City, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply. $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, $600 wildcard plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

How does Platte City compare to Kansas suburbs?

Platte City residents face Missouri s $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs have unlimited homestead and $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Kansas, you must reside there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Platte City specifically?

Platte City is the Platte County seat near KCI Airport. Rapid growth means many homes are relatively new with mortgages close to market value. If you have minimal equity, Chapter 7 may work well. Check your equity against the $15,000 exemption.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Missouri for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

## Bankruptcy in Pleasant Hill, MO - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/pleasant-hill.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Pleasant Hill, MO

Bankruptcy in Pleasant Hill, MO

Pleasant Hill is a small city at the intersection of Cass and Jackson counties, situated along Highway 7 south of Lee's Summit. It serves as a bedroom community for Kansas City with a historic downtown and growing population.

Key Filing Information

County

Cass County

Population: 8,600 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106

Vehicle$3,000

Household Goods$3,000

Wildcard$600 + up to $3,250 unused homestead

Wages75% exempt (90% head of household)

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Missouri does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Missouri state exemptions.

Someone in Pleasant Hill, MO can protect $15,000 in home equity. Someone across the state line in Kansas can protect unlimited home equity. Same metro area. Vastly different outcomes.

Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000. Kansas: $20,000 / $40,000 (married).

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Pleasant Hill

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Which bankruptcy court handles Pleasant Hill, MO cases?

Pleasant Hill is in Cass County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106.

What exemptions apply in Pleasant Hill, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply. $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, $600 wildcard plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full guide.

Pleasant Hill residents face Missouri s $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs have unlimited homestead and $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file where you live. If you move to Kansas, you must reside there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Pleasant Hill specifically?

Pleasant Hill sits near the Cass/Jackson county border. All Missouri counties use the same exemptions. The key question is whether your home equity exceeds $15,000 - if so, Chapter 13 may be the safer route.

## Bankruptcy in Prairie Village, KS - Filing Guide, Exemptions &amp; Resources - Kansas City
Source: https://kansascitybankruptcy.org/cities/prairie-village.html

Kansas City Bankruptcy Guide
HomeChapter 7Chapter 13ExemptionsCitiesCountiesFAQOBP

Home » Cities » Prairie Village, KS

Bankruptcy in Prairie Village, KS

Prairie Village is a residential suburb in northeastern Johnson County with a population of about 23,000. One of the oldest suburbs in Johnson County, it features mature neighborhoods with tree-lined streets close to the state line.

Key Filing Information

County

Johnson County

Population: 22,957 (approx.)

Federal District

District of Kansas

Kansas City, KS Division

Bankruptcy Courthouse

500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101

341 meetings and hearings held here

Zip Codes

66208

All file in D. Kan.

Kansas Exemptions in Prairie Village

PropertyKansas Exemption

HomesteadUNLIMITED (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural)

Vehicle$20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married)

Household GoodsNo specific dollar limit on necessary items

Personal Property$20,000 total (single) / $40,000 (married)

Wages75% exempt

Retirement AccountsFully exempt

Kansas does not allow federal bankruptcy exemptions. You must use Kansas state exemptions.

The Kansas Advantage

Prairie Village residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption. Across the state line in Missouri, homeowners can protect only $15,000 in home equity. This is one of the most dramatic exemption differences in the country.

Kansas vehicle exemption: $20,000 (single) / $40,000 (married). Missouri vehicle exemption: $3,000.

Full Missouri vs. Kansas comparison →

See the complete exemptions guide →

Filing Bankruptcy in Prairie Village

The Process

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the District of Kansas. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Debtor education - Complete a second financial course before discharge ($10-25).

Discharge - Chapter 7: ~60 days after the 341 meeting. Chapter 13: after completing your 3-5 year plan.

Cost Estimates

Chapter 7 filing fee: $338 (installment payments available)

Chapter 13 filing fee: $313 (can be paid through your plan)

Attorney fees: Typically $1,000-$2,000 for Chapter 7; $2,500-$4,000 for Chapter 13 in the KC area

Credit counseling: $15-25 per course (two required)

Full cost breakdown →

District Statistics

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate

31.4%

D. Kan. (2008-2024) - significantly lower than Missouri side

Compare: Missouri Side

40.4%

W.D. Mo. (2008-2024) - higher dismissal rate across the state line

View full district statistics →

Local Resources

Prairie Village City Hall - 7700 Mission Rd, Prairie Village, KS 66208

Kansas Legal Services - 712 S Kansas Ave #200, Topeka, KS 66603 (statewide intake)

Full legal aid and resource directory →

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Prairie Village, KS cases?

Prairie Village is in Johnson County, which files in the District of Kansas. The courthouse is at 500 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101. The D. Kan. has a 31.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Prairie Village, KS?

Kansas exemptions apply: unlimited homestead (up to 1 acre urban / 160 acres rural), $20,000 vehicle (single) or $40,000 (married), fully exempt retirement accounts. Kansas does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does the Kansas side compare to Missouri for bankruptcy?

Prairie Village residents benefit from Kansas's unlimited homestead exemption versus Missouri's $15,000, and Kansas's $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. The D. Kan. also has a lower Chapter 13 dismissal rate (31.4% vs 40.4%). Full comparison.

Can I use Missouri exemptions if I recently moved from Missouri?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3), if you have not lived in Kansas for at least 730 days (2 years) before filing, you may need to use the exemptions from your prior state. If you moved from Missouri to Kansas recently, you may still be subject to Missouri's lower exemptions. Consult an attorney about your specific timeline.

What about Prairie Village specifically?

Prairie Village is directly across State Line Road from Missouri. A homeowner on the Kansas side enjoys unlimited homestead protection. A homeowner one block east in Missouri gets only $15,000. Same neighborhood, vastly different bankruptcy outcomes.

Disclaimer: This page provides general educational information about bankruptcy, not legal advice. Every situation is different. Consult a qualified bankruptcy attorney licensed in Kansas for advice about your specific circumstances. This site does not recommend or endorse any specific attorney or law firm.

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Raymore, MO cases?

Raymore is in Cass County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. The W.D. Mo. has a 40.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Raymore, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does Raymore compare to Kansas suburbs for bankruptcy?

Raymore residents are subject to Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs across the state line have an unlimited homestead exemption and a $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file in the district where you live. Raymore residents must file in the Western District of Missouri. You cannot choose Kansas to get better exemptions. If you move to Kansas, you must live there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Raymore specifically?

Raymore has experienced rapid home value appreciation, which can push equity above Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Newer homeowners who purchased recently may have less equity and more Chapter 7 options.

Bankruptcy in Raytown, MO

Raytown is a suburb surrounded by Kansas City in Jackson County, with a population of about 29,200. It is centrally located in the metro area and has a mix of older and newer housing stock.

Key Filing Information

County

Population: 29,211 (approx.)

Federal District

Western District of Missouri

Kansas City Division

Credit counseling - Complete an approved course within 180 days before filing ($15-25).

File your petition - Filed with the Western District of Missouri. The automatic stay stops collections, garnishments, and foreclosures immediately.

341 meeting - About 30 days after filing, attend a brief meeting of creditors at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. Most last 5-10 minutes.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which bankruptcy court handles Raytown, MO cases?

Raytown is in Jackson County, which files in the Western District of Missouri, Kansas City Division. The courthouse is at 400 E 9th St, Kansas City, MO 64106. The W.D. Mo. has a 40.4% Chapter 13 dismissal rate.

What exemptions apply in Raytown, MO?

Missouri exemptions apply: $15,000 homestead, $3,000 vehicle, $3,000 household goods, wildcard of $600 plus up to $3,250 unused homestead. Missouri does not allow federal exemptions. Full exemptions guide.

How does Raytown compare to Kansas suburbs for bankruptcy?

Raytown residents are subject to Missouri's $15,000 homestead exemption. Kansas suburbs across the state line have an unlimited homestead exemption and a $20,000/$40,000 vehicle exemption versus Missouri's $3,000. Full comparison.

Can I file in Kansas instead of Missouri?

No. You must file in the district where you live. Raytown residents must file in the Western District of Missouri. You cannot choose Kansas to get better exemptions. If you move to Kansas, you must live there at least 730 days before Kansas exemptions apply under 11 U.S.C. section 522(b)(3).

What about Raytown specifically?

Raytown sits just minutes from the Kansas state line. A Raytown resident with a $200,000 home and $50,000 in equity can protect only $15,000 in Missouri. The same homeowner in Prairie Village, KS - 15 minutes away - would have unlimited homestead protection.
